_notes: All row/column references verified against the live template using openpyxl.
Sheet name 'Schedule A ' has a trailing space — DO NOT trim it; openpyxl matches exactly.
Working Balance A1 and A2 contain BOTH the label and example text in a single cell — see writeFormat hints.

Existing SUM formulas in the template often reference truncated ranges (e.g., =SUM(F40) instead of =SUM(F29:F42)) because the template was filled in for one example case. The app MUST regenerate SUM ranges to match the actual rows it inserts; do not preserve the literal existing formulas.

Every data section is cramped — the template has 1-15 row capacity per section. The app will almost always need to insert rows. After insertion, update the section totals AND the grand-total SUM formulas downstream (e.g., Schedule C grand total at F98 references all sub-section totals).
Schedule C grand total formula at F98 currently omits Miscellaneous. When Miscellaneous data is populated, the app must add F<misc-total-row> to the F98 formula.

The 'Insert name of Vendor' placeholder rows above each subcategory's column headers are vendor-grouping markers that staff use to organize transactions by payee. The app may insert vendor-name rows between the column-header row and the data, or just leave the placeholder and start writing data in the row beneath the column-header row.

matterName._note: Cell already contains 'Name of Case  EX. Conservatorship of...' — overwrite entire cell using this format

caseNumber._note: Cell already contains 'Court and Case No. EX. San Bernardino...' — overwrite entire cell using this format

accountingType._note: e.g. 'First Account Current', 'Second Account Current', 'First and Final Account'

fiduciaryName._note: Leading space is intentional and matches existing template formatting. Example: ' of Conservator, Wendy Smith, CLPF'

summaryHeader._note: Static label — only rewrite if the template gets cleared

period._note: Date format MM/DD/YY (e.g., ' 9/26/24 - 11/30/25'). Leading space matches existing template.

workingBalance._note: Working Balance pulls all numeric values from the schedule sheets via cross-sheet formulas. Do NOT touch column D — it's all formulas.

statements._note: Three blocks side-by-side, each 6 columns wide (B-G, I-N, P-U). Column H and O are gap columns. Add a 4th block at column W if needed for a 4th account.

blockColumns._layout: Within each 6-column block (e.g., block 1 starts at column B), the columns map as below. Substitute the block's start letter for the relative offsets.

bankTransactions._note: One block per account. The template ships with 2 example blocks; the app inserts more blocks as needed. Each block is 16 rows tall.

block._note: Add the block's startRow to each offset. Example: Block 1 startRow=2 → bank name at row 2, column headers at row 3, data rows 4-10, totals row 12, balance check row 16.

totalsFormulas._note: When inserting rows, regenerate these formulas with updated ranges.

grandTotal.labelText: Total Property on Hand at the Beginning of the Accounting Period
grandTotal._note: Row references are relative to the cash and non-cash totals rows; if those move (rows inserted), update D23/E23 formulas accordingly.

grandTotal.labelText: Total Property on Hand at the End of the Accounting Period

knownIssues: Cell E21 in the live template currently has a malformed formula: '=SUM(,E12)' — the leading comma will produce #NAME? in some Excel versions. The app should rewrite E21 to '=SUM(E12)' or '=E12' on save.

scheduleA._sheetNameNote: Trailing space is intentional and matches the live template — do not trim.
scheduleA._columnNote: Column D is intentionally blank/spacer — write nothing to D.

Interest._note: If Interest needs more than 5 data rows, insert rows before row 14 and update both the formula and downstream row references.

Pensions, Annuities, and Other Regular P.subHeaderText: Pensions, Annuities, and Other Regular Periodic Payments:
Pensions, Annuities, and Other Regular P.totalsLabelText: Total Pensions, Annuities, and Other Regular Periodic Payments:

Miscellaneous Receipts._note: Existing template has both a sub-totals cell (E42) and a final totals cell (E44 = SUM(E42)). Simplest: write totalsFormulaPattern directly to E44 and either zero out E42 or sum E42 from the data range too.

grandTotal._note: When recreating: =SUM({InterestTotalsCell},{PensionsTotalsCell},{MiscReceiptsTotalsCell}). Note the grand total currently lands in column D, not E.

Living Expenses._note: Existing F43 formula is just '=SUM(F40)' (single cell) because the template's example case had one row. App must rewrite to the full range.

Insurance._note: Insurance has nested vendor groupings (existing template shows 'Total Transamerica Life Insurance' at D84). For multi-vendor cases, expect per-vendor sub-totals; the section's grand total at F86 should sum all vendor sub-totals.

Miscellaneous._note: Template has no dedicated Miscellaneous totals row. The app must add a totals row above row 98 (the grand total) and update F98 to include the Misc total. The label text in the template is 'Miscellanous' (typo missing the second 'e') — preserve as-is or fix; flag with the firm.

grandTotal._note: Current formula OMITS Miscellaneous. When recreating: include the Misc subcategory totals cell. Pattern: =SUM({allSubcategoryTotalsCells}).

scheduleF._columnNote: Columns D and E are intentionally blank/spacer in the description area — only B, C, and F carry data. The Description header at C6 visually spans C through E.

adHocSchedules._note: Schedules added per case as new sheets. App injects skeleton on demand. Use the same column layout as Schedule C for D/G/P (transaction-style) and Schedule F for K (property-style). I/L (trade or business) typically need a custom skeleton — flag for staff. X (Cash Reconciliation) is reconciliation-only and gets no Working Balance line.

D.label: Losses on Sales During the Period

I.label: Net Income from Trade or Business

L.label: Net Loss from Trade or Business
